title: Schur–Erdős Problem on R(p; m)
statement_text: Is it true that $R(p; m) = 2^{O(m)}$ for every fixed $p \geq 3$?
context_text: Schur (1916) proved the existence of $R(3; m)$ and established $\Omega(2^m) \leq R(3; m) \leq O(m!)$. It is a major open problem in Ramsey theory, for which Erdős offered prize money, to close the gap; the current lower-bound record is $R(3; m) \geq \Omega(3.199^m)$ due to Xiaodong et al.
paper_contribution: Proves that $R_{d,t}(p; m) = 2^{O(m)}$ for every fixed $p \geq 3$ and fixed $d, t \geq 1$ when each color class is defined by a semi-algebraic relation of complexity at most $t$, and shows the order of magnitude is tight.
notes: Described as an open problem in prose without a labelled conjecture environment; PDF source — math may be garbled.

title: Erdős–Shelah Problem on multicolor clique-coloring thresholds
statement_text: For positive integers $p \geq 3$ and $2 \leq q \leq \binom{p}{2}$, determine how the minimum number of colors $m$ needed so that any $m$-coloring of $K_n$ forces every $p$ vertices to span at least $q$ distinct colors depends on $n$, $p$, and $q$.
context_text: Erdős and Shelah posed a general Ramsey-type problem asking for the minimum number of colors in an edge-coloring of $K_n$ such that every $p$ vertices induce at least $q$ colors. The paper introduces $f_{d,t}(n,p,q)$ as the semi-algebraic analogue and applies the multicolored regularity lemma (Theorem 1.2) to resolve the threshold.
paper_contribution: Solves the semi-algebraic variant: for fixed $d, t \geq 1$ and $p \geq 3$, proves $f_{d,t}(n, p, \lceil \log p \rceil + 1) \geq \Omega(n^{1/(c\log^2 p)})$ and (for $t \geq 4$) $f_{d,t}(n, p, \lceil \log p \rceil) \leq O(\log n)$, precisely locating the transition from logarithmic to polynomial in $n$.
notes: The original Erdős–Shelah problem statement is not reproduced verbatim; inferred from the paper's description of the semi-algebraic variant. PDF source — math may be garbled.
